action.reasoning: Observation: current failure is entirely checkpoint-schema mismatch in predict.py: xlsr_ft.pt contains model_state plus model_kwargs, but the actual weights are baseline-style attentive pooling keys (bn/head/pool.attn), while current code instantiates SpoofDetector and loads strict=True. Analysis: we already have a very strong checkpoint (dev_eer 0.00263) and phase-1 eval previously succeeded, so the highest-value action is to stop changing training and make model.py/predict.py exactly support both saved schemas. We should build explicit BaselineDetector and SpoofDetector constructors, infer architecture from state_dict keys, load xlsr_ft.pt into the baseline-style model when its keys indicate that, verify locally on both checkpoints, and immediately submit eval to avoid ending with zero. Decision: rewrite model.py and predict.py compactly with schema-aware checkpoint loading, run local smoke tests for both checkpoints, then submit xlsr_ft.pt.
action.command: python predict.py --data_path tmp_pred_data --checkpoint checkpoints/xlsr_ft.pt --output tmp_pred.json && python predict.py --data_path tmp_pred_data --checkpoint checkpoints/baseline.pt --output tmp_pred.json
